stories.title: Europe’s industrial wake-up call
stories.summary: The July revision of the EU ETS Directive will determine whether the EU can reconcile it with industrial competitiveness. In short, whether it can achieve decarbonization without deindustrialization. EU’s global competitiveness gap is growing Europe now operates in a very different environment than when its climate policy was originally designed. According to forecasts from the Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD) and the International Monetary Fund (IMF), EU economies ar

stories.title: Dangerous temperatures forecast for central and eastern Europe
stories.summary: Red warnings issued as record-breaking heatwave pushes east, with authorities urging people to stay indoors Parts of central, eastern and southern Europe sweltered on Monday as the “heat dome” behind last week’s record-breaking temperatures shifted east, bringing dangerous conditions to a new swathe of the continent. Budapest is forecast to exceed 40C on Tuesday, according to models from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts. Belgrade and Bucharest reached 38C and 37C respective

stories.title: Szerdától csak mobillal lehet fizetni a parkolásért Budapesten
stories.summary: De többe is kerül majd.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Drámai a költségvetés állapota, a valós hiány meghaladná a 8 százalékot
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök beszámolt a kihelyezett kormányülésről, ahol szó volt az átvilágítások első eredményeiről, a kiemelt beruházások sorsáról, a közmédia helyzetéről és a következő hónapok ütemtervéről is.

stories.title: Ütött az óra a kedvezményes devizaváltásban, egymásra licitálnak a bankok
stories.summary: Már az idővel is versenyeznek a bankok az ügyfelekért a kedvezményes devizaváltásban. Nem is olyan régen erre egyáltalában nem volt lehetőség idehaza, mostanra azonban egymásra licitálva kínálják a hazai bankok. Ebben le is körözték a Revolutot, amelynél először ismerhették meg a magyarok ezt a kedvezményt. A harc folytatódik: egyre feljebb emelik a kedvezményes kereteket, az Erste pedig júliustól átállítja az órát.

stories.title: Gyártástól a polcig – így támogatja a technológia a termékértékesítést (x)
stories.summary: A mai fogyasztók számára a termék értéke számos tényező összessége, mint például a hatékonyság, a kényelmes csomagolás, az információgazdag kommunikáció és a termék állandó elérhetősége ott és akkor, amikor szükség van rá. Ezek a tényezők együttesen jelentősen befolyásolják, hogy a fogyasztók hogyan érzékelik a termék értékét. A P&G megérti ezeket az elvárásokat, és fejlett eszközök segítségével válaszol rájuk. Emilia Burzyńska, a Procter & Gamble európai adatvédelmi igazgatója elmagyarázza, hog

stories.title: Az országgyűlés legmeglepőbb összecsapása: Forsthoffer Ágnes vs Magyar Péter – élőben közvetítettük és elemeztük a parlamentet
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a költségvetés drámai állapotáról beszél, és személyesen irányítja a hőség elleni védekezést. A hétvégi Pride tanulságai, kánikulai parlament élőben: közvetítés és elemzés László Róberttel és Mizsur Andrással.

stories.title: Július elejétől bővül az éjszakai közlekedés a budapesti agglomeráció felé
stories.summary: Július 1-jétől új vagy sűrűbb éjszakai kapcsolatot kap Érd, Vác, Balassagyarmat, Gyömrő és Vecsés térsége .

stories.title: Magyar Péter nem hagyta szó nélkül, hogy Forsthoffer Ágnes elvette tőle a szót
stories.summary: Meglepő pengeváltás a házelnök és a miniszterelnök között. Szijjártó Péter megjelent az ülésteremben. Percről percre a parlament hétfői ülése.

stories.title: Elhagyta a látogatóknak kijelölt utat, onnan zuhant le a magyar gyerek a bledi vár közelében
stories.summary: A vár területének üzemeltetője szerint nem a kijelölt ösvények egyikén történt a baleset.

stories.title: Furcsa autó bukkant fel Helsinkiben, a sofőr magyarul káromkodva küldte el az újságírót, aki közel ment hozzá
stories.summary: A Xiaomi egy helyi lapnak megerősítette, hogy az ő megbízásukból járja az autó az utcákat, megígérték, hogy kivizsgálják az esetet.

stories.title: László Róbert: Magyar Péter nem véletlenül most kezdett a költségvetés állapotáról beszélni
stories.summary: László Róbert, a Political Capital választási szakértője szerint a Tisza gyors alkotmánymódosításai hordoznak politikai kockázatokat. A videós elemzés összefoglalója.

stories.title: Kétezer tonna PET-palack és darálék maradt az egykor prosperáló magyar cég után
stories.summary: A készleteken 130 millió forintért adna túl a cég felszámolója, de az ár lehet, hogy csak elírás. Különösen, hogy az ajánlat első ránézésre nem túl vonzó.

stories.title: Enzo Maresca lett a Manchester City új vezetőedzője
stories.summary: Eddig is nyílt titok volt, hogy egykori mentoráltja váltja majd a tíz év után távozó Pep Guardiolát, most hivatalos lett a dolog.

stories.title: Videón, ahogy Forsthoffer Ágnes elveszi a szót Magyar Pétertől
stories.summary: Magyar Péter fel akarta használni a felszólalási időkeretét, hogy egy korábbi felszólalásra is válaszoljon, de ilyet a házelnök szerint nem tehetett.

stories.title: Hungary: Magyar's 'Operation Purgatory' tackles Orban system
stories.summary: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The focus: fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

stories.title: Száguldanak a magyar fizetések euróban, és sok cég nagyon nem örül ennek
stories.summary: Amíg gyengült a forint, a multik aránylag könnyen kigazdálkodták a gyorsan növekvő béreket. A tendencia megfordulása kikényszerítheti a gazdaságpolitikai fordulatot, amire a kormány amúgy is készült.

stories.title: Katasztrófába torkollott a kísérlet, ami először akart megszelídíteni egy hurrikánt
stories.summary: A negyvenes években felvetődött az ötlet, hogy felhőetetéssel el lehet csendesíteni a hurrikánokat. Egy csapat kutató meg is próbálta: több millió dolláros kár és egy emberélet lett az ára.

stories.title: Heatwave breaks records in Germany, Denmark and Czech Republic
stories.summary: An estimated 150 million people have been experiencing temperatures of over 35C across Europe.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
stories.summary: Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern Europe Temperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues . In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared

stories.title: Putin admits Ukrainian drone strikes are driving Russian fuel shortages
stories.summary: President says attacks on infrastructure are causing ‘obvious’ problems but that they are ‘not critical’ Vladimir Putin has admitted Russia is facing fuel shortages as Ukraine steps up its long-range drone campaign, with repeated strikes setting oil refineries ablaze and forcing multiple regions to introduce unprecedented petrol rationing. Speaking to Russian state television late on Sunday, the Russian president acknowledged for the first time that Ukrainian attacks on energy infrastructure wer

stories.title: Supreme court rules Trump can fire regulators but finds against him in Fed governor and E Jean Carroll cases – live
stories.summary: Justices expand president’s power to fire regulators after ruling Fed governor’s firing was unconstitutional and declining bid to overturn sexual abuse verdict Key cases today: Trump can fire federal regulators ; Lisa Cook firing unconstitutional ; bid to appeal $5m E Jean Carroll verdict ; mail-in ballots arriving after election day The supreme court is due to release some of its final opinions at 10am ET, with major decisions including on Donald Trump ’s effort to end birthright citizenship an

stories.title: US and Iran agree to resume talks after weekend of strikes
stories.summary: The U.S. and Iran agreed Sunday evening to halt attacks in the Gulf and restart negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz after a weekend of strikes threatened their less-than-two-week-old ceasefire. Technical talks on the June 17 memorandum of understanding are expected to resume this week. Citing a U.S. official, Axios reported both sides had agreed to “stand down for now” and allow commercial vessels to move through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has not publicly confirmed the agreement. The move to

stories.title: No ‘one size fits all’ answer on AI and jobs in Europe, OpenAI chief economist says
stories.summary: European Union countries need to develop their own plans to address the impact of artificial intelligence on the labor market, OpenAI’s chief economist said on Monday. There is no “one size fits all answer for AI” across the bloc, Ronnie Chatterji said, speaking at POLITICO’s AI and the future of work event . European countries are trying to figure out how to grapple with the expected ripple effects of AI on jobs and employment. Across the bloc, Germany, Greece and Italy

stories.title: Kushner, Witkoff to lead peace talks in Doha after weekend strikes
stories.summary: U.S. and Iranian officials will meet in Doha on Tuesday, President Donald Trump said, just days after a series of military exchanges strained the standing memorandum of understanding between Washington and Tehran. Trump announced the meeting Monday morning on Truth Social, saying that Iran had requested it. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed on “Fox & Friends” that special envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff would travel to Qatar for “high-level meetings” regarding the

stories.title: Brussels claps back at Trump’s tech threats
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission fired back Monday at Donald Trump’s fresh tariff threats against Europe’s tech rules, just as EU and U.S. officials opened talks in Washington meant to repair their increasingly strained digital relationship. A delegation led by the EU’s top tech official, Roberto Viola, is in Washington until Wednesday for what the Commission is calling a “dialogue on a future potential dialogue” with its U.S. counterparts. The visit comes after the U.S. president

stories.title: EU plans reset with Serbia as elections loom
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU is drafting plans to reward Serbia for dialing back controversial laws, in order to incentivize the country to stop backsliding on democracy. Last week, Serbia’s parliament backed relaxing controversial rules that international legal experts had warned would undermine the independence of the judiciary and weaken the fight against organized crime. The European Commission is now looking for ways to incentivize Belgrade to continue reforms and avoid it reneging on commitment

stories.title: Liberal frontbencher calls for party ‘rebrand’ after drop in polls, prompting ridicule from Labor
stories.summary: Melissa McIntosh’s comment comes after Angus Taylor’s claim Morrison government ‘breached trust’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Liberal frontbencher Melissa McIntosh has said the party might need a “rebrand” to win back voters who continue to desert the opposition, with the Coalition hitting a new historic polling low. Her comments came after the opposition leader, Angus Taylor, claimed the Morrison government –

stories.title: Trump claims Iran has agreed to hold peace talks in Doha after recent clashes
stories.summary: US president posts that meeting will take place on Tuesday in Qatari capital after exchange of fire in strait of Hormuz Donald Trump has claimed that Iran has agreed to hold talks in Doha after the US and Iran traded fire in the strait of Hormuz this weekend threatening the collapse of a ceasefire meant to keep open the strait of Hormuz and pave the way for peace talks. In a terse post on Truth Social, the US president claimed that the meetings would take place in the Qatari capital, as US media

stories.title: South Africa braces for anti-migrant protests
stories.summary: Tensions are rising ahead of planned anti-migrant protests on June 30, as South Africa's government warns against violence. Analysts say deep social and economic crises are fueling unrest and xenophobia.

stories.title: Brussels downplays Vatican’s criticism of EU double standard on war
stories.summary: The EU on Monday defended its commitment to international law after the Vatican accused Brussels last week of applying a double standard when it comes to war. Speaking at Pope Leo XIV’s closed-door conference on war last Friday, Cardinal Víctor Manuel Fernández, the Vatican’s doctrinal chief, argued that governments have increasingly been invoking international law based on political convenience rather than universal principles. He accused the bloc of inconsistency in its foreign pol

stories.title: La France réfléchit à de nouvelles taxes européennes pour financer le budget de l’UE
stories.summary: BRUXELLES — Emmanuel Macron a chargé le gouvernement de trouver de nouvelles taxes européennes pour financer le prochain budget à long terme de l’UE , qui s’élèvera à 2 000 milliards d’euros, selon cinq responsables publics au fait des discussions. Sans accord sur de nouvelles taxes, Paris serait contraint de verser davantage d’argent à Bruxelles, ce qui donnerait des arguments au Rassemblement national, actuel favori des sondages à l’élection présidentielle. Cette initiative française intervien

stories.title: A Ukrainian military school graduation ceremony – photo essay
stories.summary: For most teenagers, the last day of school is about summer, freedom and new beginnings. At a military school in western Ukraine, it marks the end of years spent living, studying and training together – and, for many, the first step towards a career in the armed forces at a time of war The school in western Ukraine is home to about 400 cadets from across the country. On paper, it resembles any other high school, where students study mathematics, physics, English and history before taking the same

stories.title: Escalating US-Iran strikes threaten interim peace agreement
stories.summary: Tehran attacks Bahrain and Kuwait amid efforts to open strait of Hormuz without Iran’s direct oversight Fresh hostilities in Gulf suggest US-Iran memorandum was too broadly worded A new round of escalating strikes between Iran and the US has continued, further undermining the fragile interim peace agreement between the two countries, and prompting Donald Trump to threaten violence that would ensure Iran “will no longer exist”. On Sunday, Tehran launched drone and missile attacks against Bahrain

stories.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill dozens in eastern Afghanistan
stories.summary: Pakistan says strikes were aimed at a terrorist group while Taliban condemn ‘cowardly act of aggression’ Pakistani airstrikes in three eastern provinces of Afghanistan killed 36 civilians and wounded 163 others, Afghan officials have said, as attacks between the two countries showed no sign of abating. Pakistan’s information minister, Attaullah Tarar, said the operations on Sunday night were aimed at a terrorist group his country blamed for a deadly militant attack in Karachi that killed three s

stories.title: US supreme court rules Trump’s firing of Lisa Cook from Fed was unconstitutional
stories.summary: Justices find president does not have constitutional authority to fire Federal Reserve governor without cause US supreme court decisions – live updates Sign up for the Breaking News US email The US supreme court has refused Donald Trump ’s attempts to immediately fire a Federal Reserve governor, in a landmark ruling that limits a president’s authority over the central bank. In a 5-4 opinion, the court said that Lisa Cook can stay on as a governor while she fights unproven allegations of mortgage

stories.title: US supreme court rules Trump can fire leaders of independent agencies
stories.summary: Case focused on White House’s March 2025 firing of Federal Trade Commission member Rebecca Slaughter US supreme court decisions – live updates Sign up for the Breaking News US email The US supreme court ruled that Donald Trump can fire leaders of independent agencies or commissions, ending 90 years of court precedent that curbs executive power. The vote in the case of Trump v Slaughter is 6-3, with dissents from Justices Sonia Sotomayor, Ketanji Brown Jackson and Elena Kagan. Continue reading...

stories.title: Shooter kills five adults at youth welfare facility in Stade, Germany
stories.summary: Two arrested after incident at town west of Hamburg, with motive unknown Five people have been shot and killed at a youth welfare facility ⁠in a northern German town, police have said. The motive for ⁠the ​incident in Stade, close to the port city of Hamburg, on Monday was not immediately clear, a police ⁠spokesperson said. Two people, including the suspected ⁠shooter, had been arrested, he added. Continue reading...

stories.title: Andy Burnham confirms he will set up ‘No 10 North’ in Manchester in major policy speech – UK politics live
stories.summary: Makerfield MP and hopeful prime minister says he will ‘bring about biggest rebalancing of power the country has ever seen’ Andy Burnham vows to set up No 10 North as ‘nerve centre of rewired Britain’ Asked what she thought of Andy Burnham not taking questions this morning, Badenoch said: Andy Burnham doesn’t like questions. stories.title: Five people killed in shooting in northern Germany, police say – Europe live
stories.summary: German police say two people were apprehended after ‘numerous’ shots were fired at a youth care facility in Stade Meanwhile, Ukraine is bracing to absorb the impact of the heatwave on its energy network, already pummelled by Russian attacks over more than four years of war, AFP reported. stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes aftershock hits near capital city as man rescued from rubble after being trapped for 106 hours – latest updates
stories.summary: No damage reported in 4.6 magnitude aftershock; At least 1,450 people are known to have died in initial quakes but number is expected to rise In an update to X, El Salvador’s president has said that after hours of intensive work rescuers have freed Aaron Levi Cantillo Vargas, 21, who was trapped under a building in Caraballeda, La Guaira, calling the operation “a miracle”. “This rescue was made possible thanks to the coordinated effort of the rescue teams from Venezuela, Mexico, and El Salvador,

stories.title: Germany news: Temperatures plunge after historic heat wave
stories.summary: A record-breaking heat wave has moved on, with temperatures sinking rapidly amid many storms. German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul is heading to Washington for talks with his US counterpart, with NATO issues in focus.

stories.title: In Slovenia, Pride meets growing support — and hostility
stories.summary: One of the first steps taken by the new right-wing government in June was to remove the rainbow flag outside the Culture Ministry. As Pride Month draws to a close, DW surveys the situation of Slovenia's LGBTQ+ community.

stories.title: Can Thailand, Cambodia unlock massive oil and gas reserves?
stories.summary: An estimated $300 billion in oil and gas reserves is sitting under overlapping territory in the Gulf of Thailand. Cooperation is complicated by political tensions over a separate land border dispute.

stories.title: Australia and Vanuatu sign deal barring foreign military base on Pacific island
stories.summary: China formed policing ties with Vanuatu in 2023 and has donated equipment including drones, patrol boats and vehicles to its police force.

stories.title: Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan
stories.summary: The Taliban government says civilians were killed, while Pakistan maintains they targeted militants.

stories.title: Thai airline employee arrested in Australia on suspicion of smuggling €302,000 of heroin
stories.summary: The 26-year-old woman was on duty on board an international flight when she arrived at Melbourne Airport on Thursday.

stories.title: DR Congo bans mass gatherings in the capital to prevent spread of Ebola
stories.summary: Opposition politicians accuse the government of using the outbreak to halt a planned protest.

stories.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump's appeal of E Jean Carroll's sexual abuse case
stories.summary: The decision means the US president will now have to pay her the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.

stories.title: Thousands of migrants queue for repatriation in South African camps
stories.summary: Over 15,000 Malawians are being repatriated from crowded Durban camps as South Africa enforces migration rules and regional governments organise returns.

stories.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, officials say
stories.summary: Pakistan carried out airstrikes overnight in eastern Afghanistan, Islamabad said on Monday, adding its operations had targeted militants as the Afghan government reported dozens of civilian casualties.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch tries out her Andy Burnham attack lines
stories.summary: LONDON — Agent of “chaos” or more of the same? Britain’s opposition parties are still trying to work out how to paint Andy Burnham. With the Labour MP likely just weeks away from becoming Britain’s prime minister, Conservative Leader Kemi Badenoch used a punchy speech Monday to warn of a “summer of chaos” under his leadership. “Investment decisions will be put on hold,” the Tory leader told a Westminster press conference. “Every time the news

stories.title: Bruce Lehrmann loses bid to get footage from documentary featuring Brittany Higgins ahead of rape trial
stories.summary: Judge says ‘no legitimate forensic purpose’ to former Liberal staffer’s subpoena requesting footage, ahead of his Queensland trial Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former political staffer Bruce Lehrmann has lost a legal bid to access copies of all footage from a documentary featuring Brittany Higgins. Lehrmann had subpoenaed the producers of the documentary Silenced and signalled a possible attempt to ban its broadcast before his Queensland trial for rape. Continue r

stories.title: Done Quixote? Film archivists on quest to finish Orson Welles passion project
stories.summary: Team hope 30 of hours of footage held by three countries will be enough to bring to life film-maker’s vision More than 70 years after he shot the first few frames, Orson Welles’s ambitious project to put Don Quixote on the big screen may finally be completed thanks to a consortium of European film archivists. Oja Kodar, the American film-maker’s partner and collaborator, has given her blessing to the project led by archives in France, Spain and Italy, along with the Munich film museum, to produc

stories.title: Weather tracker: North-west US hit by snow ahead of eastern heatwave
stories.summary: Western states experience unseasonally low temperatures but New York and Washington DC could reach 40C by end of week Unseasonal snow has fallen in some parts of the US, while other parts of the country brace for a heatwave this week. A strong cold front spread into the western US from the northern Pacific over the weekend, bringing an abrupt change in conditions to a region that had been experiencing high summer temperatures amid drought. Temperatures from the Canadian border to California have

stories.title: The choice before Europe: the law of the strongest or reparative justice?
stories.summary: European nations are starting to pay attention after a landmark UN resolution, but reparations is about more than symbolic remembrance or returning stolen artefacts • Ghana conference calls for formal apology for transatlantic slave trade In Accra, Europe finally showed up to a conversation it can no longer avoid. For four years, the global movement for reparative justice has been gathering political momentum across Africa and the Caribbean, from Nairobi to Bridgetown, from Accra to Addis Ababa.

stories.title: Europe heatwave shows need to reject climate denial ‘lies’, says EU green chief
stories.summary: Teresa Ribera blames ‘ideologically driven’ falsehoods, driven by those with vested interests in fossil fuels, for attacks on green policy The heatwave wreaking chaos across Europe is a “dramatic warning” to reject climate naysayers, a European Commission vice-president says. Teresa Ribera, executive vice-president for a clean, just and competitive transition, lambasted those who listened to the “vested interests” of the fossil fuel industry rather than scientists and their own citizens. stories.title: Shares in chipmakers underpinning AI boom rocket in first half of 2026
stories.summary: Value of some chip manufacturers have tripled, or more, driving Asia Pacific stock markets sharply higher Shares in chipmakers have surged in the first half of this year as investors piled into companies that make the hardware underpinning the AI boom, according to analysis. Investors have driven up the value of semiconductor and memory chip manufacturers, whose profits have soared during 2026, at the expense of some large software companies, which have fallen out of favour this year. stories.title: Man murdered two London women in predatory sexual attacks, court told
stories.summary: Trial begins of Simon Levy, who denies killing Carmenza Valencia-Trujillo and Sheryl Wilkins and attacking third woman A man murder​ed two women and left a third for dead in a series of predatory sexual attacks over an eight-month period​, a jury has been told. Simon Levy, 40,​ denies killing Carmenza Valencia-Trujillo, 53, on 17 March 2025 in south-east London​ and Sheryl Wilkins, 39, on 24 August 2025 in Tottenham, north London. He also denies attacking a third woman, aged 35 at the time, on 2

stories.title: Stalker who used fake Tinder profile to lure men to ex’s home jailed for eight years
stories.summary: Asad Hussain, 36, from Cheadle in Greater Manchester, found guilty of stalking involving serious alarm or distress A man has been jailed for eight years after he set up a fake Tinder profile for his ex-partner and enticed multiple men to her house with the intention of raping her. Asad Hussain, 36, also known as Ash Hussain, from Cheadle in Greater Manchester, appeared at Chester crown court on Monday, where he was also handed a 15-year restraining order. stories.title: Love Island contestant reportedly leaves show over link to stabbing
stories.summary: Gabriel Garland, 24, was involved in New Year’s Eve altercation but not charged with any offence A Love Island contestant has reportedly left the programme over his connection to a stabbing in London. Gabriel Garland, 24, was removed from the ITV2 dating series after briefly featuring in an episode on Sunday night as a Casa Amor villa boy. Continue reading...

stories.title: US military races to vaccinate new recruits before flu shots expire
stories.summary: Move comes amid growing outbreak in Texas and after two-month halt on mandatory shots The US military is racing to vaccinate new recruits after a two-month halt on mandatory flu shots – but it’s a temporary reprieve, as the shots will soon expire and new doses will not be available for months. Officials will need to lean on other prevention measures to contain the growing flu outbreak at Lackland air force base in San Antonio, Texas, experts say. Continue reading...

stories.title: Andy Burnham picks Greater Manchester CEO to oversee devolution of power at No 10 North
stories.summary: Caroline Simpson worked closely with Burnham when he was Greater Manchester mayor UK politics live – latest updates Andy Burnham has asked the current chief executive of the Greater Manchester combined authority to be his deputy chief of staff, leading the new No 10 North, if as expected he becomes prime minister in three weeks’ time. stories.title: US supreme court rules geofence warrants require constitutional privacy protections
stories.summary: Law enforcement’s use of warrants sweeping smartphone location data requires privacy protections, court rules US supreme court decisions – live updates The US supreme court has ruled that law enforcement’s use of sprawling warrants that sweep up smartphone location data requires privacy protections under the fourth amendment, in a boost to critics who view their use as an unconstitutional dragnet. Justice Elena Kagan wrote the majority opinion, which held that the sensitive data scooped up by “g

stories.title: Venezuela earthquake: father and son found alive in rubble after four days as death toll nears 1,500
stories.summary: President says ‘we always hold onto hope’ as discovery of earthquake survivors spurs fresh search efforts despite dwindling chances of survival Mortuary in Caracas ‘overwhelmed’ as Venezuela struggles to respond after earthquakes A man and his teenage son were found alive under the rubble in Venezuela on Sunday, in a town about 40km north of the capital Caracas, AFP journalists reported, as the death toll from last week’s twin earthquakes passed 1,450. stories.title: US, Iran agree to halt strikes but broader talks still to begin
stories.summary: A US official said both the US and Iran have agreed to pause their attacks and allow vessels to move through the Strait of Hormuz. Technical talks between both sides are still to resume.

stories.title: Australia-Vanuatu deal bars foreign bases in island nation
stories.summary: Australia and Vanuatu have signed a deal seen as a bid to check China's growing security presence in the region. But an economic deal between Vanuatu and China is still in the works.

stories.title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
stories.summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, and the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease of life.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquake: Aid ramps up as rescue window closes
stories.summary: Venezuela was shaken by an aftershock on Monday as rescues continued. Interim president Delcy Rodriguez said she remained hopeful about rescue operations as criticism of her government grows. DW has the latest.

stories.title: EIB announces largest-ever loan: €3 billion to Airbus
stories.summary: The European Investment Bank (EIB) has said the loan, the largest in its history, is aimed at helping boost the EU planemaker's research efforts. The money will be directed to commercial and defense projects.

stories.title: Germany: At least 6 killed in shooting in Stade
stories.summary: A large police operation is underway in the northern German city following gunfire that left several people dead. Authorities have arrested two suspects.

stories.title: US-India trade deal: Inside the key sticking points
stories.summary: Both India and the US have signaled that the first phase of a landmark trade deal is close. DW looks at the main challenges in getting a deal past the finish line.

stories.title: UK-Africa relations: Starmer's 'reset' that never delivered
stories.summary: Back in 2024 British Prime Minister Keir Starmer promised a fresh UK–Africa partnership. Two years later, his resignation leaves unfinished ambitions, scaled-back aid and questions about Britain's long-term commitment.

stories.title: Serbia: Protests continue after Vucic says he will step down
stories.summary: Student-led demonstrations continued even after President Vucic announced his plan to resign within weeks. For more than 18 months, demonstrators have kept Vucic under pressure.

stories.title: Pakistan says it struck militant targets in Afghanistan
stories.summary: The strikes come a day after an attack in the southern city of Karachi killed three paramilitary troops. Afghanistan's ruling Taliban condemned the "brutal" and "cowardly" strikes, saying they killed scores of civilians.

stories.title: How population decline is exposing Germany's old divides
stories.summary: Decades after the fall of the Berlin Wall, a population slump in the east lays bare the existing divisions

stories.title: Delivery drivers, unsung heroes who kept Qatar going during Iran war
stories.summary: As missiles threatened the Gulf and residents stayed indoors, delivery drivers kept delivering food and medicine across Doha, with many dubbing them heroes.

stories.title: First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer
stories.summary: The fossil, collected in Antarctica in 1985, is part of the tail of a beast called a Titanosaur.

stories.title: Venezuela search teams scramble as hope fades of finding earthquake survivors
stories.summary: Experts say the first 72 hours after natural disasters define the narrow window for rescuing the living. After that the search usually becomes one of recovering bodies.

stories.title: Brussels marks 250th anniversary of US independence with concerts, fireworks and drone show
stories.summary: Thousands gathered in Brussels for the US Embassy's "Freedom 250" celebration, marking the 250th anniversary of American independence with senior Belgian, US and NATO officials in attendance.

stories.title: One killed in shooting at California World Cup fan zone, San Jose police say
stories.summary: The shooting happened at San Pedro Square, one of several places in the San Francisco Bay Area where crowds have gathered to watch World Cup matches on big outdoor screens.

stories.title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
stories.summary: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.

stories.title: Watch: Lightning hits Eiffel Tower during violent storm in Paris
stories.summary: Lightning struck the Eiffel Tower as a powerful thunderstorm swept across Paris on Saturday night, sending visitors scrambling for shelter. The dramatic weather followed days of record-breaking heat in France, where authorities say the extreme temperatures contributed to a sharp rise in deaths.

stories.title: Heatwave: Berlin police use water cannon to cool Bruno Mars fans
stories.summary: Berlin police used a water cannon to cool thousands of Bruno Mars fans queuing outside the Olympiastadion as temperatures soared during Europe's heatwave.

stories.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders on fame, pressure and World Cup fever
stories.summary: As the Netflix show following their lives returns, the squad open up about their new-found fame.

stories.title: Morocco fans light up Monterrey before Netherlands knockout clash
stories.summary: Fans of Morocco and Mexico fill Monterrey’s Macroplaza with songs and celebrations ahead of Morocco’s World Cup Round of 32 clash with the Netherlands.

stories.title: Aerial images reveal scale of destruction after Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Four days after twin quakes hit Venezuela, rescues continue at Los Cocos beach, with 1,500 dead, 189 buildings down and a father and son pulled alive.

stories.title: BBC at Venezuela baseball stadium sheltering earthquake survivors
stories.summary: The survivors are hopeful the authorities will find them some form of temporary accommodation while the country rebuilds - for now they are dependant on donated food and clothing.

stories.title: Teyana Taylor's tears and Lauryn Hill tribute steal show at BET Awards
stories.summary: Taylor had an emotional reaction to being presented with the Icon of the Year award by Janet Jackson.

stories.title: Biden blasts Trump 'vanity projects', calling him a 'loser' at fundraising event
stories.summary: The former US president took aim at his successor's projects in Washington DC, including a planned new White House ballroom.

stories.title: Senior South African police officer survives assassination attempt
stories.summary: Deputy crime intelligence boss Maj-Gen Feroz Khan was due to appear before an inquiry into police corruption this week.

stories.title: Australian charged with murder after Thai teen's body found in suitcase
stories.summary: Australian man Simon Carman has been charged with murdering 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla after her body was found inside a suitcase in Pattaya. Police say the investigation is ongoing, while the suspect denies abducting and killing the teenager.

stories.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook
stories.summary: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.

stories.title: Four women and man shot dead at youth welfare centre in northern Germany
stories.summary: Two people have been arrested, one of whom is the suspected shooter, local police say.

stories.title: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.title: Australian man charged with murder after dead girl found in suitcase in Thailand
stories.summary: The body of Tunchanok Donhomla, 17, was found discarded near a railway track on Saturday, police say.

stories.title: Iran and Oman hold first Hormuz talks as US-Iran deal stays on edge
stories.summary: Iran's deputy foreign minister said the first Joint Hormuz Committee meeting took place in Muscat, after multiple US media outlets reported that fresh talks between the two sides were planned for Tuesday, a claim Tehran has since shot down, prompting Trump to counter by saying it would go ahead.

stories.title: Donald Trump threatens to annihilate Iran after crossfire over Hormuz – as it happened
stories.summary: Iran attacked Bahrain and Kuwait after US strikes, and threatened a ‘complete halt’ to talks US and Iran trade strikes as both sides accuse the other of endangering ceasefire We will soon be closing this liveblog, but you’ll be able to stay up-to-date with our ongoing coverage of the Middle East here. Here is a summary of today’s events: Iran launched drone and missile attacks Sunday targeting Bahrain and Kuwait in response to US airstrikes that hit the Islamic Republic , and threatened a “compl

stories.title: Pakistan's Geo News apologizes for Muharram broadcast
stories.summary: The broadcaster Geo News was handed a 15-day suspension for a documentary that was "liable to hurt the religious sentiments of viewers," Pakistan's media watchdog said.

stories.title: Germany's development aid strategy under fire
stories.summary: Days ahead of the launch of the North-South Commission, development aid organizations have urged the German government to reverse billions in cuts to development aid.

stories.title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
stories.summary: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.

stories.title: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
stories.summary: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.

stories.title: Watch: Inside the Venezuela country club now a makeshift hospital
stories.summary: Survivors of Venezuela's devastating earthquakes are being cared for at a makeshift hospital formed on the site of a country club in Caraballeda.

stories.title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
stories.summary: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.

stories.title: Iran threatens total halt to talks amid intensive US air activity near Hormuz
stories.summary: The IRGC said the weekend US strikes violated the framework deal and warned that violating vessels would face a "crushing response," as Euronews journalists in Doha observed US refuelling aircraft taking off towards Hormuz in the same formation as the previous night's strikes.

stories.title: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C
stories.summary: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.

stories.title: Joy as man pulled from rubble three days after Venezuela quake
stories.summary: Spanish and international rescue teams pulled a survivor from the rubble four days after deadly earthquakes struck Venezuela. Search operations continue as crews race to find more survivors, with the death toll rising to more than 1,400.

stories.title: Loved and loathed: The making of India's viral liver doctor
stories.summary: Dr Cyriac Abby Philips has built a large online following, but often clashes with India's traditional medicine specialists.

stories.title: Anger directed at Venezuela's official response to earthquake recovery
stories.summary: Residents in quake-affected areas have expressed anger over the state's efforts to locate survivors and help victims, the BBC's Orla Guerin reports.

stories.title: Extrém forrósággal indul a hét, sokfelé már hajnalban 26 fokot mértek
stories.summary: Napközben akár 40 fok is lehet, a legmagasabb szintű hőségriasztás van érvényben.

stories.title: A FIFA kiszámolta, hogy 45 kilométernyi hot dogot ettek már meg a foci-vb szurkolói
stories.summary: Sorra dőlnek a rekordok a foci-vb-n: 4,6 millió néző, 99,7 százalékos jegyértékesítés és 215 gól a csoportkörben.

stories.title: Az Egyesült Államok és Irán újrakezdi a tárgyalásokat a Hormuzi-szorosról
stories.summary: Közben ismét emelkedik az olaj ára.

stories.title: Vízpótlás nélkül a Velencei-tónak nem lesz kiszámítható jövője
stories.summary: Rekordkevés víz van a Velencei-tóban, akár át is sétálhatunk rajta. Kérdés, megmenthető-e még, és hogy tisztított szennyvízzel vagy a Dunával töltsük-e fel.

stories.title: Neymar már készen áll arra, hogy 15 percnél többet játsszon egy meccsen
stories.summary: A brazilok kapitánya, Ancelotti azt mondta, hogy úgy készülnek a japánok ellen, mintha döntőt játszanának. Pedig még csak a legjobb 16 a tét.

stories.title: Meghosszabbították szerda éjfélig a legmagasabb hőségriasztást, a Tisza legalacsonyabb állását mérték, Pest megyében több helyen akadozik a vezetékes vízellátás
stories.summary: Eljött a nap, amikor a perzselő napsütésé lesz a főszerep országszerte, vagyis még mindig a hőkupola alatt ülünk, talán majd szerda este fog megváltást hozni zivatarokkal. Folyamatosan frissülő tudósításunkban percről percre követjük a rendkívüli időjárást.

stories.title: 24.hu: 900 milliónyi forintnyi NKA-s pénz jutott Fidesz-közeli szervezetekhez négy megyében
stories.summary: Békés megyében összesen 309,9 millió forintnyi, Jász-Nagykun-Szolnok megyében 216,5 millió forintnyi NKA-s támogatást találtak.

stories.title: Bár Vučić belengette a lemondását, folytatódnak a tiltakozások Szerbiában
stories.summary: A tüntetők attól tartanak, a köztársasági elnök valójában nem akarja átadni a hatalmat, csak pozíciót váltana.

stories.title: Száguldva menekült a rendőrök elől a teherautót elkötő 15 éves, végül egy szántón fogták el az M3-asnál
stories.summary: A fiú Debrecenből vitte el a teherautót, Szabolcsig jutott vele. Az autó néha a 180 km/órás sebességet is elérte.

stories.title: Van pár jel, ami arra mutat, hogy Algéria és Ausztria is a mindkettejüknek kényelmes döntetlenre játszott
stories.summary: A két csapat 3-3-as döntetlent hozott össze, amivel Iránt kiejtették, Algéria pedig elkerülte a spanyolokat, pedig egy győzelemmel bosszút állhatott volna.

stories.title: Sikertelenül küzdött a Főkert a túlhasználat ellen, visszaadja a Szabadság tér üzemeltetését a főváros az V. kerületnek
stories.summary: Az önkormányzat nem csökkentette a rendezvényi terhelést, ezért Bardóczi Sándor szerint a Főkert hiába próbálta jól karbantartani a teret.

stories.title: Kármán András bejelentette a NAV új elnökét
stories.summary: Az adószakmai elnökhelyettest, Tamásné Czinege Csillát kérték fel a pozícióra. Az ő helyére is javasoltak valakit.

stories.title: Operation of Chinese battery plant in Debrecen suspended over serious environmental concerns
stories.summary: Samples of groundwater taken near the Semcorp plant showed that the level of aluminum is more than thirteen thousand times higher than permitted.

stories.title: Mi történt a venezuelai földrengés hátterében?
stories.summary: Szakértővel elemezzük, miért nem lehet az ilyesmit pontosan előre jelezni, és miért fontos mégis megérteni a természeti katasztrófa geológiai alapjait.

stories.title: Egy hely Baranyában, ahol öt napig egy kicsit másképp működik a világ (x)
stories.summary: Néha az az érzésünk, hogy egyre nehezebb valóban jelen lenni. Mire odafigyelnénk valamire, már jön is a következő hír, a következő vélemény, a következő történet. Az Ördögkatlan tizenkilenc éve arra emlékeztet, hogy léteznek még helyek, ahol az emberek leülnek egymás mellé beszélgetni. A 19. Ördögkatlan Fesztivál idén is ezt kínálja: öt napot, amikor a színház, a zene, az irodalom, a film és a képzőművészet nem külön műfajok, hanem egy közös történet részei.

stories.title: Már perzsául is olvasható A fehér király, Dragomán regényének Iránban is fontos az üzenete
stories.summary: A fordítója szerint az, ahogy Dragomán egy elnyomó hatalom uralma alatt élő, megkínzott országról ír, tükrözi a saját aggodalmait.

stories.title: Csoportos létszámleépítést jelentett be a Richter Gedeon Nyrt.
stories.summary: A létszámcsökkentés a gyógyszergyártási területeket érinti.

stories.summary: A férfi a rendőröknek azt állította, önvédelemből cselekedett.

stories.summary: Legalább száz tűzoltó segíti az oltást.

stories.title: Pakisztán ismét légicsapásokat mért Afganisztánra, a tálibok szerint legalább 36 civil halt meg
stories.summary: Ismét kiéleződött a konfliktus a két ország között.

stories.title: Kedden lejár a határidő az Alaptörvény-ellenes klímatörvény módosítására
stories.summary: Gajdos László minisztériumának egy napja maradt lépni.

stories.title: Spanyolország a csoportmeccsek nagy vesztese, az argentinok esélyei megugrottak
stories.summary: A kisebb csapatok közül a legnagyobbat a házigazda amerikaiak léptek előre, de nőtt az esélye a végső győzelemre Mexikónak és Kolumbiának is.

stories.title: 53 millió forintba került az MTVA-s Papp Dánielék négy külföldi útja
stories.summary: A legdrágább egy 2025. áprilisi út volt, amikor négyfős delegációval utazott Las Vegasba. Erre 16,5 millió forintot költöttek.

stories.title: Tanuljunk a mamutoktól meg a polipoktól. Úgy leszünk igazán emberek
stories.summary: Ormányosba ültetett emberi agy, szuperintelligens polipkolónia, tengert pásztázó MI-halászhajó. Ray Nayler könyveiben az ember mindig nagyot álmodik, de csak a jövőt torzítja.

stories.title: 31 fokos a Ráckevei-Duna holtága, szórványos halpusztulást figyeltek meg
stories.summary: A horgászszövetség 80-100 darab nagyobb haltetemet számolt össze a növényzettel fedett vízterületen.

stories.summary: A dohányipari cég szerint a kirúgások egy olyan program részét képezik, amelynek célja, hogy a vállalat rugalmasabbá, költségtudatosabbá és innovatívabbá váljon.

stories.title: Egy Ferrari 458 Spidert fogtak a rendőrök a tompai határ közelében
stories.summary: Az autót a német hatóságok körözik.

stories.title: Egy ifjúsági központ közelében lövöldöztek Észak-Németországban, legalább öten meghaltak
stories.summary: Egy gyanúsítottat elfogtak.

stories.title: Teljesült a kívánsága, feltűnhet a több mint hatvan éve futó General Hospitalban John Oliver
stories.summary: A brit humorista pár hónapja viccelődött azzal, hogy szívesen játszana az ikonikus szappanoperában.

stories.summary: A volt miniszter szerint ő és a munkatársai is mindig jóhiszeműen jártak el.

stories.summary: Az EU-ban nagyjából minden tizedik ember nehezen jut elegendő, biztonságos és tiszta ivóvízhez. Ez a probléma is Cipruson a legsúlyosabb.

stories.title: Brazília az esélyes, de ha beakad egy saslövés, azt Alisson Becker sem szedi ki a hálóból
stories.summary: Azon túl, hogy a Tsubasa kapitány már 30 éve megjósolta a ma esti brazil–japán-párosítást, nagyon sokat köszönhet neki a japán és a nemzetközi futball is.

stories.title: Hiába ment Trump a Legfelsőbb Bíróságig, nem másítják meg az elvesztett szexuális zaklatós ügyének ítéletét
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnököt 2023-ban 5 millió dolláros kártérítésre ítélték, mert az esküdtszék szerint zaklatta és rágalmazta E. Jean Carroll újságírót.

stories.title: Venezuelan earthquakes test Trump’s new western hemisphere policy after gutting of USAID
stories.summary: Marco Rubio is scrambling to provide effective disaster response to country whose president US deposed in January This week’s dual earthquakes in Venezuela are a test for the new era of American power in the western hemisphere, as the Trump administration scrambles to provide an effective disaster response mission to a country that it now calls an ally in Latin America, after a US special forces raid in January deposed the country’s strongman leader, Nicolás Maduro. stories.title: Fresh hostilities in Gulf suggest US-Iran memorandum was too broadly worded
stories.summary: Document appears to have been subject to conflicting interpretations on key issues of Lebanon ceasefire and strait of Hormuz Middle East crisis live – latest updates The sudden eruption of fresh hostilities in the Gulf – just 10 days after Iran and the US signed a memorandum of understanding to end the conflict – threatens to put the two countries back on the path to war . It appears the deliberately opaque wording in the memorandum has been unable to withstand the pressure of conflicting interp

stories.title: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
stories.summary: The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

stories.title: Uganda military chief shuts media outlets, slams 'free press'
stories.summary: Muhoozi Kainerugaba said that he issued the order as he does not "believe in a free press." He is the son of President Yoweri Museveni, who has held his grip on power for decades.

stories.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
stories.summary: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

stories.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

stories.title: US and Iran trade strikes amid claims of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: The US also carried out strikes on Iran on Friday after a drone attack on the M/V Ever Lovely ship. US aircraft targeted Iranian missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites, CENTCOM said.

stories.title: Iraqi political officials arrested on corruption charges in overnight raid
stories.summary: Iraqi security forces sealed off all entrances to the capital’s heavily fortified Green Zone early Sunday and carried out raids inside the compound that houses key government institutions and foreign embassies.

stories.title: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
stories.summary: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

stories.title: Bahrain says it was targeted by Iranian drones after US strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Iranian state media reported that strikes hit a telecommunications mast in the Sirik area of southern Iran.

stories.title: UK maritime agency raises Strait of Hormuz threat level after oil tanker reports being struck
stories.summary: It comes after the US military carried out strikes on Iran, which it said were in response to Iranian drone attacks on shipping in the Strait.

stories.title: New Caledonia polls open in first local vote in the French territory since 2019
stories.summary: Election will determine balance of power in New Caledonia before fresh negotiations with France on the territory’s status Polls opened in the French overseas territory of New Caledonia on Sunday for the archipelago’s first provincial elections since 2019, after the vote was delayed as talks stalled over its political future. stories.title: Shadow war: how use of proxy forces by Iran, Israel and US is driving Middle East instability
stories.summary: Across region there is a push to disarm militia and reinforce state authority but the temptation to use proxies remains As Marco Rubio ended his brief visit to the Middle East on Friday, he sought to cast in the best possible light his discussions with leaders of the Gulf states. Those leaders are deeply anxious that the deal agreed earlier this month between Iran and the US fails to address their worries about continued Iranian efforts to project power and influence throughout the region. “They

stories.title: Australian man charged with murder in Thailand after teenage girl’s body found in suitcase
stories.summary: Simon Peter Carman, 46, has denied the charges against him Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An Australian man has been charged with the murder of a teenage girl whose naked body was found in a suitcase in Thailand. stories.title: Kanada kilencven percet átaludt, aztán egy késői villanással a 16 között találta magát
stories.summary: Az Eustáquio góljával Dél-Afrikát búcsúztató társházigazdák még soha nem jutottak vb-n ilyen messzire.

stories.title: Gyurcsány Ferenc a vereségek természetéről mereng
stories.summary: Tapasztalat az van. Bár azt ígérte nem fog, most mégis újra politizál egy kicsit.

stories.title: Albania's 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind the protests?
stories.summary: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

stories.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
stories.summary: As a heat wave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

stories.title: Germany news: New heat record at 41.7 degrees Celsius
stories.summary: For the third consecutive day, the DWD German Weather Service has reported a record temperature. The worst of the heat is moving east and it should start cooling soon.

stories.title: Russia's Putin promises security as Ukraine ramps up attacks
stories.summary: The Russian leader condemned what he called "terrorist attacks on our territory and infrastructure facilities." Ukraine has targeted oil refineries and weapons factories, causing massive disruption in recent weeks.

stories.title: Europe send search and rescue personnel to Venezuela in response to massive quakes
stories.summary: The European Union has begun sending aid to Venezuela through its Civil Protection Mechanism in response to Wednesday's earthquakes of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude, which have left more than 50,000 people missing, according to the UN.

stories.title: Israel's Netanyahu welcomes 'historic' Lebanon agreement as Hezbollah rejects it
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu declared his country's agreement with Lebanon "a blow to Iran and Hezbollah", while the militant group's leader Naim Qassem declared it void.

stories.title: Panic attacks and fractures - Venezuela hospital treats earthquake victims
stories.summary: The BBC travelled to a Caracas hospital that is treating people from the worst affected areas.

stories.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban
stories.summary: Children under the age of 16 have been prevented from 10 key social media platforms in Australia since December 2025.

stories.title: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

stories.title: Millie Bobby Brown and Louis Partridge on belly laughs, sibling vibes and Enola Holmes
stories.summary: Brown talks to the BBC about having her "hands and heart" all over the detective adventure series.

stories.title: 117 dead dogs found at California 'no-kill' animal rescue - many with gunshot wounds
stories.summary: Hundreds more animals are still missing from the California facility as investigators continue excavating the grounds.

stories.title: Sort Your Life Out: The four most common cluttering mistakes and how to fix them
stories.summary: Tidying expert Dilly Carter shares her best tips on organising your kitchen, lounge and clothes.

stories.title: Bad Bunny lights up London with history-making stadium show
stories.summary: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.

stories.title: More than 68,000 people still missing in Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
stories.summary: A preliminary estimate from the United Nations Development Programme put the cost of direct physical damage caused by the quakes at $6.7 billion.

stories.title: Algerians celebrate as dramatic draw seals World Cup knockout spot
stories.summary: "We wanted to take our revenge against Austria": Algeria fans react in Algiers after the draw against Austria secured the Fennecs' place in the World Cup round of 32.

stories.title: Spain removes Gibraltar from its tax haven list after 35 years, adds Russia
stories.summary: The Spanish Ministry of Finance has updated its list of non-cooperative jurisdictions, removing Gibraltar after 35 years and adding Russia, in line with the European Union's position

stories.title: Israel formally recognises Armenian World War I 'genocide'
stories.summary: Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar said Israel had "fulfilled a moral duty by recognizing the historical truth, and rejecting attempts to deny it".

stories.title: Canada defeats South Africa 1-0, as World Cup knockout stage begins: Here is all you need to know
stories.summary: France have made a mightily impressive start to their campaign, scoring 10 goals in victories over Senegal, Iraq and Norway. Canada defeated South Africa 1-0 on Sunday, sending South Africa home.

stories.title: Uganda's leading media outlets shut down by army chief
stories.summary: NTV and Daily Monitor say their offices in the capital are under "military siege".

stories.title: Australian man arrested in Thailand after 17-year-old’s body found in suitcase
stories.summary: The 46-year-old was stopped at about 9.30pm on Friday while preparing to board a Jetstar flight to Perth, according to police and local media Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An Australian man has been arrested at a Thai airport in connection with the alleged murder of a 17-year-old girl whose naked body was found in a suitcase, according to local police. The man , 46, was stopped at about 9.30pm on Friday while p

stories.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
stories.summary: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

stories.title: Venezuela updates earthquake toll: 920 dead, 3,360 injured
stories.summary: National Assembly president Jorge Rodríguez updated the figure on Friday. The UN reports over 50,000 missing. Spain urges its citizens in Venezuela to contact consular emergency services.

stories.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building
stories.summary: Police and emergency crews sealed off the area around Beijing's tallest skyscraper after a small plane crash damaged the building. Images from the scene showed a large hole in the upper floors of the 528-metre CITIC Tower, while witnesses reported seeing aircraft debris at the base of the building.

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to 1,430 as searches continue for survivors
stories.summary: More than 520 emergency responders from across the EU have been mobilised to support search and rescue efforts in Venezuela, the European Commission said.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: The earthquake in Venezuela, a brutal heatwave in Europe, the resignation of Keir Starmer and the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Europeans voice concerns amid sweltering heatwave - video
stories.summary: European residents and tourists voiced their concerns over soaring temperatures as the continent continues to grapple with 'the most severe and widespread' heatwave ever. In summer 2022, more than 60,000 people died due to heat in Europe, and the current heatwave is already disrupting lives and livelihoods European heatwave is worst ever and impossible without climate crisis, scientists say Continue reading...

stories.title: Do you really need to speak German to take a cooling dip? This row in Halle raises all manner of red flags | Fatma Aydemir
stories.summary: A pool manager invoked safety to bar non-German speakers during the heatwave. With the far right soaring, the move is making everyone less safe Humans are vulnerable in water. Beaches have red flags; swimming pools have flashy warning signs to remind us of our vulnerability when we just want to cool down in the midst of a searing heatwave. Pool rules are essential, especially when children are around, or tourists who don’t know about the local safety measures. With pictograms and whistling lifeg

stories.title: Profound lessons from dog training, the story of the Brexit campaign and France’s struggle with heat-trap homes
stories.summary: Need something brilliant to read this weekend? Here are six of our favourite pieces from the last seven days Continue reading...

stories.title: People in Britain used to agree to disagree. Since Brexit, they no longer dare to talk about difficult things | Elif Shafak
stories.summary: Studies suggest the country is more divided than ever – but we won’t come together unless we begin to talk rationally and calmly Read more from the Brexit Vote: 10 Years On series When I first moved to England, nearly two decades ago, I was invited to attend a talk in London on “the future of British identity”. It was a heated debate from the start, and it became all the more intense when the subject of putting colonial history in the school curriculum was raised. The two main speakers held oppo

stories.title: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
stories.summary: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

stories.title: 'Help, we are trapped': Venezuela earthquake victims turn to Euronews' TikTok to beg for rescue"
stories.summary: Lack of resources and patchy telecoms have driven many Venezuelans to despair. In this context, outlets’ social networks, including Euronews, have become key platforms for appeals for help.

stories.title: Israel declassifies Entebbe raid documents on eve of 50th anniversary
stories.summary: Secret documents reveal Israeli officials privately weighed negotiations before ordering the daring 1976 Uganda hostage rescue operation.

stories.title: Marco Rubio announces Israel-Lebanon framework deal, hailing it as first step toward peace
stories.summary: The officials did not disclose details of the deal, which was signed by Yechiel Leiter and Nada Hamadeh, Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors to the United States.

stories.title: IRGC denies direct hotline with US, as Trump warns of ceasefire violations in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The spokesperson for the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) denied claims by American officials regarding the establishment of a direct hotline between Iran and the United States concerning the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a "sheer lie".

stories.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
stories.summary: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

stories.title: Belgium through to World Cup knockout stage as Iran forced to wait
stories.summary: Iran now face an anxious wait to see if they'll progress to the round of 32 after their final group game finished 1-1 against Egypt.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: striking satellite images before and after
stories.summary: Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes last Thursday, with the La Guaira area particularly hard hit. Here is a series of before-and-after images showing the scale of the disaster.

stories.title: Woman rescued after car plunges into flooded sinkhole
stories.summary: A police officer rescued a woman after her car fell into a sinkhole caused by a burst water main in Virginia, US. Bodycam footage shows bystanders helping steady the officer as he pulled the driver to safety.

stories.title: Anthropic cleared to restore Mythos 5 access to certain US organisations
stories.summary: "We’re restoring access for these organizations quickly, and we’re continuing to work with the government to expand access to Mythos 5 and make Fable 5 available for general use again," the company said on Friday.

stories.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Which third-place teams will reach the knockout stage?
stories.summary: The eight best third-place sides are determined first by points and then by goal difference, with the final standings to be decided on Saturday night.

stories.title: What Keir Starmer's resignation means for European security
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?
